summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic Toolbar core component (issue #276) including state machine, roving tabindex, keyboard navigation, RTL/vertical routing, and separator attrs, with full test coverage.
summary.outcome: PR #701 was opened against main, the snapshot-reviewed label was applied, CI ran (snapshot check failed due to a labeling race and was rerun), and a Codex review P1 bug (roving tabindex not seeded on fresh toolbar) was identified and fixed with a follow-up commit.

repo: The repo uses a `snapshot-reviewed` label that must be applied to PRs before CI runs the Snapshot Change Check workflow; missing it causes a predictable CI failure unrelated to snapshot content.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment and signals activity with a 👀 reaction; results appear as review threads rather than issue comments.
The spec lives at spec/components/layout/toolbar.md and must be kept in sync with implementation; cargo xtask spec validate enforces this.
The project uses cargo insta for snapshot testing with --unreferenced=reject enforced on i18n feature builds.
cargo xci --profile fast is the fast integration check command for the project.

code.finding: SetItems/item registration did not seed the roving tabindex target for a freshly-created enabled toolbar, leaving every item at tabindex=-1 until explicit focus. Fix: seed first enabled rendered item during registration when no valid current target exists.

workflow: GitHub Actions won't rerun a failed job until the entire workflow run completes; attempting early rerun silently does nothing.
When a label-gated CI check fails due to a race (label applied after workflow triggered), the correct fix is to wait for the full run to finish, then rerun only the failed jobs.
Co-authoring Codex-suggested fixes with 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>' is the established convention in this repo.

friction: The snapshot-reviewed label must be manually applied after PR creation; there is no automation to do it pre-CI, causing a predictable labeling-race CI failure on every PR that touches snapshots.
GitHub's rerun-failed-jobs API silently no-ops if the workflow is still in progress, requiring a poll-and-wait loop before the rerun actually takes effect.
Codex review results are not surfaced as issue comments, requiring a GraphQL query against reviewThreads to detect completion and read findings.
open_items: Verify that the rerun of the Snapshot Change Check CI job passes after the labeling race fix.
Confirm Codex review is satisfied after the roving tabindex seed fix is pushed.
Consider automating snapshot-reviewed label application in CI or via a pre-push hook to eliminate the labeling race.
